title: elymus churchii
intro: Elymus churchii, also known as Church's wild rye, is a species of grass native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Elymus churchii are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Elymus churchii is typically prepared as an herbal tea or infuser, and can also be used in tincture or salve form to harness its medicinal properties.
